page: Microsoft Azure E8_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 50 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 205 GB of local storage, runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks position the E8_v3 in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. In CPU-intensive workloads, the server recorded a stress-ng single-core score of 804.19 and a multi-core score of 3703.28. Geekbench testing yielded a single-core score of 811.0 and a multi-core score of 3030.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show read-only small block speeds of 277.61 GB/sec and large block speeds of 35.42 GB/sec. Database operations on Passmark scored 2095.61, while Redis SET operations achieved 4.4 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8_v3 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance CPU throughput. However, users face a clear tradeoff: while the 64.0 GB RAM capacity is generous, the older Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor delivers poor single-core and weak multi-core performance. This server is best suited for legacy enterprise applications, relational databases with moderate transaction rates, memory-intensive caching layers, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E8_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz, this server features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 50 MB L3 cache. It includes 205 GB of local storage but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 804.19 and a multi-core score of 3703.28, placing its compute capabilities in the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth is measured at 35.42 GB/sec for large blocks, and Redis SET operations reach 4.4 million operations per second. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the E8_v3 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v3 is an Ev3-family VM with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 205 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 205 GB of local storage for database and caching needs
Delivers moderate performance suitable for memory-bound applications
Best fit for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise apps
